description: Replace the resource-name-only document-reference failure surface in `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.External/Backend/UpsertResult.cs` and `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.External/Backend/UpdateResult.cs` with a path-aware per-occurrence contract for DMS-982. Acceptance: the new contract can represent every failing document-reference instance with its concrete JSON path, target resource, and an internal reason enum at minimum covering `Missing` and `IncompatibleTargetType`; the contract is usable by both POST and PUT handlers; and descriptor failure contracts remain available for existing 400-style mapping.
steps-to-verify: Add or update unit tests that construct the new result types and confirm every failing occurrence can be represented without collapsing to unique resource names.

description: Update `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Handler/UpsertHandler.cs`, `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Handler/UpdateByIdHandler.cs`, and any shared response helpers so path-aware document-reference failures become 409 responses keyed by concrete JSON path instances. Acceptance: existing status semantics stay unchanged, every failing path instance is emitted in `validationErrors`, duplicate referential ids at different JSON locations produce multiple entries, and descriptor failures continue to map to 400 validation responses.
steps-to-verify: Add handler tests covering POST and PUT document-reference failures with multiple concrete paths for the same missing referential id.

description: Add the reusable relational write-prerequisite resolver service and contracts for DMS-982 without wiring a new relational `IDocumentStoreRepository` path. Acceptance: the service accepts request-scoped extracted references plus `MappingSet`/resource context, performs per-request memoization and dedupe, preserves per-occurrence diagnostics in a `ResolvedReferenceSet`, and exposes a narrow adapter seam so future auth/write prerequisite batching can append SQL without redesigning the service contract.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for request-scoped dedupe and memoization behavior using fake adapter results.

description: Implement concrete reference-path addressing helpers that map Core-emitted `DocumentReference.Path` values to compiled document-reference bindings and stable ordinal addresses needed by later flattening work. Acceptance: helpers resolve root, collection, and nested-collection reference paths against a compiled write plan; reject unknown, mismatched, or wildcard paths deterministically; and produce a per-request `(binding, ordinalPath)` index entry for each resolved reference occurrence.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for root, single-collection, and nested-collection reference paths, including invalid-path failures.

description: Implement metadata helpers that derive allowed target `ResourceKeyId` sets for document-reference validation from the runtime mapping set and derived relational model. Acceptance: concrete targets resolve to exactly one allowed `ResourceKeyId`, abstract/superclass targets resolve to the allowed concrete member keys for the hierarchy, descriptor resources are excluded from document-reference compatibility checks, and missing mapping-set metadata fails fast with deterministic errors.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests covering concrete targets, abstract targets, and missing-resource failures using compiled mapping fixtures.

description: Implement the PostgreSQL bulk referential-id lookup adapter for the resolver using set-based SQL against `dms.ReferentialIdentity`, `dms.Document`, and `dms.Descriptor`. Acceptance: document references are deduped for lookup but returned per occurrence, no per-reference query loop is introduced, descriptor membership rows are joined in the same prerequisite pass, concrete-target compatibility is validated before a resolved `DocumentId` is returned, and abstract/superclass references honor alias rows while still validating membership against allowed concrete resource keys.
steps-to-verify: Add focused unit tests for SQL shape selection and result classification for missing, found, and incompatible-target rows.

description: Implement the SQL Server bulk referential-id lookup adapter with parameter-limit handling suitable for DMS-982. Acceptance: shared resolver semantics match PostgreSQL, small lookup sets use a simple parameterized strategy, large lookup sets switch to a threshold-safe bulk strategy instead of risking the 2,100-parameter limit, descriptor membership/type checks are supported, and no per-reference query loop is introduced.
steps-to-verify: Add focused unit tests for small-list and large-list strategy selection and result classification parity with the PostgreSQL adapter.

description: Implement shared failure classification over adapter results for both document and descriptor references. Acceptance: unresolved document references and wrong-type document matches surface through the new path-aware document-reference contract with internal reasons kept distinct for tests/logging, descriptor failures distinguish at least `Missing` versus `DescriptorTypeMismatch`, and every failing concrete JSON path instance is preserved even when the underlying lookup key was deduped.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests that exercise mixed document/descriptor failures and repeated failing paths sharing the same deduped key.

description: Register the DMS-982 resolver and its dialect-specific adapter seams in backend composition without wiring a new relational repository path. Acceptance: PostgreSQL service registration can resolve the prerequisite resolver when `UseRelationalBackend` is enabled, SQL Server tests can instantiate the SQL Server adapter through the same contract/factory seam, and no `IDocumentStoreRepository` request-path integration is added in this story.
steps-to-verify: Add or update DI/composition tests that resolve the prerequisite resolver and adapter factory from the configured service collection.

description: Add a focused relational write-prerequisite integration harness that provisions only the core DMS schema plus the minimal emitted relational objects needed to exercise DMS-982 on both engines. Acceptance: the harness can seed `dms.ResourceKey`, `dms.Document`, `dms.ReferentialIdentity`, `dms.Descriptor`, and any required abstract identity tables/member tables from a compact fixture; it does not depend on the legacy JSON repository path or full E2E environment; and it can be reused by both PostgreSQL and SQL Server resolver tests.
steps-to-verify: Add shared test helpers that provision a compact fixture database for resolver tests on both dialects.

description: Add focused PostgreSQL integration coverage for the resolver service. Acceptance: tests cover request-local dedupe versus per-path diagnostics, missing document references at multiple concrete JSON locations, concrete-target mismatch, abstract/superclass alias resolution, descriptor membership failure, descriptor discriminator mismatch, and at least one large lookup batch that still resolves in bulk.
steps-to-verify: Ensure the PostgreSQL integration test connection string is configured for `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration`.

description: Add focused SQL Server integration coverage for the resolver service with parity to PostgreSQL where the query strategy differs. Acceptance: tests cover the same success/failure matrix as PostgreSQL, include at least one threshold-crossing large-list case that exercises the SQL Server bulk strategy, and assert that missing and type-mismatch outcomes match PostgreSQL at the public-result level.
steps-to-verify: Ensure the SQL Server admin connection string is configured for `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration`.
